cluster_id.name: On/Off
cluster_id.summary: Manages device on/off state. Supports Lighting (LT), DeadFrontBehavior (DF), and OffOnly features. Interacts with Level Control commands: `IF OnOff=FALSE THEN OnTime=0;` `IF OnOff=TRUE AND OnTime=0 THEN OffWaitTime=0;`

classification.summary: Provides fundamental application-level control at the endpoint scope, managing the basic on/off state of a device. It's a base cluster for simple state management.

revision_history.description: Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added; CCB 1555
FeatureMap global attribute support with Level Control and Lighting feature
New data model format and notation
Addition of Dead Front behavior and associated FeatureMap entry
Addition of OffOnly feature and associated FeatureMap entry

features.name: Lighting
features.summary: Behavior that supports lighting applications. On receipt of a Level Control cluster command that causes the OnOff attribute to be set to FALSE, the OnTime attribute SHALL be set to 0. On receipt of a Level Control cluster command that causes the OnOff attribute to be set to TRUE, if OnTime is 0, server SHALL set OffWaitTime to 0.

features.name: DeadFrontBehavior
features.summary: Device has Dead Front behavior. When supported, device exhibits 'dead front' behavior when OnOff attribute is FALSE. Other clusters MAY respond with INVALID_IN_STATE, change attributes to best-effort values. Report Transactions continue. Starts on OnOff TRUE to FALSE, stops on FALSE to TRUE. Attribute changes due to dead front SHALL NOT be skipped.

features.name: OffOnly
features.summary: Device supports the OffOnly feature. When supported, the Off command SHALL be supported and the On and Toggle commands SHALL NOT be supported. Useful for devices which can only be turned off via commands due to regulatory requirements.

values.name: AcceptOnlyWhenOn
values.summary: Indicates a command is only accepted when in On state.

values.name: Off
values.summary: Set the OnOff attribute to FALSE

values.name: On
values.summary: Set the OnOff attribute to TRUE

values.name: Toggle
values.summary: If the previous value of the OnOff attribute is equal to FALSE, set the OnOff attribute to TRUE. If the previous value of the OnOff attribute is equal to TRUE, set the OnOff attribute to FALSE (toggle).

values.name: DelayedOffSlowFade
values.summary: 50% dim down in 0.8 seconds then fade to off in 12 seconds

values.name: DyingLightFadeOff
values.summary: 20% dim up in 0.5s then fade to off in 1 second

attributes.name: OnOff
attributes.summary: Indicates whether the device is turned off (FALSE) or turned on (TRUE).

attributes.name: GlobalSceneControl
attributes.summary: Prevents storing an 'all-devices-off' situation as a global scene or destroying current settings by recalling a global scene. Set to TRUE after commands causing OnOff to be TRUE (e.g., On, MoveToLevel(WithOnOff), RecallScene, OnWithRecallGlobalScene). Set to FALSE after OffWithEffect.

attributes.name: OnTime
attributes.summary: Specifies the length of time (in 1/10ths second) the On state is maintained before automatically transitioning to Off when using OnWithTimedOff. Writing only affects when in Timed On state.

attributes.name: OffWaitTime
attributes.summary: Specifies the length of time (in 1/10ths second) the Off state is guarded to prevent another OnWithTimedOff command. Writing only affects when in Timed On state followed by Delayed Off, or in Delayed Off state.

attributes.name: StartUpOnOff
attributes.summary: Defines the desired startup behavior of a device when powered, reflected in the OnOff attribute. If null, OnOff is set to its previous value. Otherwise, behavior is defined by StartUpOnOffEnum. Does not apply to OTA reboots. Pseudocode: IF StartUpOnOff == null THEN OnOff = previous_OnOff_value ELSE OnOff = value_defined_by_StartUpOnOffEnum.

commands.name: Off
commands.summary: Turns the device off. If the OnTime attribute is supported, it is reset to 0.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
set OnOff attribute := FALSE
if OnTime attribute is supported then
    set OnTime attribute := 0
end if

commands.name: On
commands.summary: Turns the device on. If the OffOnly feature is supported, the command is rejected. If OnTime and OffWaitTime are supported and OnTime is 0, OffWaitTime is also set to 0.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
if OffOnly feature is supported then
    send_response(UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND)
else
    set OnOff attribute := TRUE
    if OnTime attribute is supported AND OffWaitTime attribute is supported then
        if OnTime attribute == 0 then
            set OffWaitTime attribute := 0
        end if
    end if
end if

commands.name: Toggle
commands.summary: Toggles the device's on/off state. If the OffOnly feature is supported, the command is rejected. Adjusts OnTime or OffWaitTime based on the new OnOff state.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
if OffOnly feature is supported then
    send_response(UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND)
else
    if OnOff attribute == FALSE then
        set OnOff attribute := TRUE
    else
        set OnOff attribute := FALSE
    end if

    if OnTime attribute is supported AND OffWaitTime attribute is supported then
        if OnOff attribute == FALSE AND OnTime attribute == 0 then
            set OffWaitTime attribute := 0
        end if
        if OnOff attribute == TRUE then
            set OnTime attribute := 0
        end if
    end if
end if

commands.name: OffWithEffect
commands.summary: Allows devices to be turned off using enhanced fading effects. Interacts with the GlobalSceneControl attribute to manage global scene storage.

fields.name: EffectIdentifier
fields.summary: Specifies the fading effect to use when turning the device off. SHALL contain one of the non-reserved values listed in EffectIdentifierEnum.

fields.name: EffectVariant
fields.summary: Indicates which variant of the effect, specified in EffectIdentifier, SHOULD be triggered. If not supported, the default variant is used. Dependent on EffectIdentifier, values from DelayedAllOffEffectVariantEnum or DyingLightEffectVariantEnum.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
check GlobalSceneControl attribute
if GlobalSceneControl attribute == TRUE then
    store current settings in global scene
    set GlobalSceneControl attribute := FALSE
    set OnOff attribute := FALSE
    if OnTime attribute is supported then
        set OnTime attribute := 0
    end if
else if GlobalSceneControl attribute == FALSE then
    set OnOff attribute := FALSE
end if

commands.name: OnWithRecallGlobalScene
commands.summary: Recalls the settings stored when the device was last turned off. If GlobalSceneControl is TRUE, the command is discarded. Otherwise, the Scenes Management cluster recalls its global scene.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
if GlobalSceneControl attribute == TRUE then
    discard_command()
else if GlobalSceneControl attribute == FALSE then
    call Scenes_Management_cluster.recall_global_scene()
    update OnOff attribute based on recalled scene
    set GlobalSceneControl attribute := TRUE
    if OnTime attribute is supported AND OffWaitTime attribute is supported then
        if OnTime attribute == 0 then
            set OffWaitTime attribute := 0
        end if
    end if
end if

commands.name: OnWithTimedOff
commands.summary: Turns devices on for a specific duration with a guarded off duration. Subsequent commands update the period. Includes logic for conditional processing and periodic attribute updates.
commands.timing: Attributes are updated every 1/10th second until OnTime and OffWaitTime are 0.

fields.name: OnOffControl
fields.summary: Contains information on how the server is to be operated. The 'AcceptOnlyWhenOn' bit specifies if the command is processed unconditionally or only when the OnOff attribute is TRUE.

fields.name: OnTime
fields.summary: Used to adjust the value of the OnTime attribute, specifying the duration the device should remain on.

fields.name: OffWaitTime
fields.summary: Used to adjust the value of the OffWaitTime attribute, specifying a guarded off duration.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
if OnOffControl.AcceptOnlyWhenOn == 1 AND OnOff attribute == FALSE then
    discard_command()
else if OffWaitTime attribute > 0 AND OnOff attribute == FALSE then
    set OffWaitTime attribute := MIN(OffWaitTime attribute, OffWaitTime field)
else
    set OnTime attribute := MAX(OnTime attribute, OnTime field)
    set OffWaitTime attribute := OffWaitTime field
    set OnOff attribute := TRUE
end if

if OnTime attribute != 0xFFFF AND OffWaitTime attribute != 0xFFFF then
    // Start periodic update
    loop every 1/10 second until (OnTime attribute == 0 AND OffWaitTime attribute == 0)
        if OnOff attribute == TRUE AND OnTime attribute > 0 then
            decrement OnTime attribute
            if OnTime attribute == 0 then
                set OffWaitTime attribute := 0
                set OnOff attribute := FALSE
            end if
        else if OnOff attribute == FALSE AND OffWaitTime attribute > 0 then
            decrement OffWaitTime attribute
            if OffWaitTime attribute == 0 then
                break // terminate the update loop
            end if
        end if
    end loop
end if

State Description.behavior: OnTime attribute is decremented every 1/10th second, unless its value equals 0xFFFF.
State Description.pseudocode: if OnTime != 0xFFFF then every 100ms: OnTime := OnTime - 1

State Description.behavior: OffWaitTime attribute is decremented every 1/10th second, unless its value equals 0xFFFF.
State Description.pseudocode: if OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF then every 100ms: OffWaitTime := OffWaitTime - 1

references.name: FeatureMap
references.description: Feature bitmap attribute, whose bits define supported features like Lighting, DeadFrontBehavior, and OffOnly. Not fully defined here.

references.name: Level Control cluster
references.description: Another cluster whose commands interact with the On/Off cluster, specifically affecting OnTime and OffWaitTime attributes. Not defined here.

references.name: OnTime attribute
references.description: An attribute of the On/Off cluster, set to 0 when OnOff is FALSE by Level Control commands. Not fully defined here.

references.name: OffWaitTime attribute
references.description: An attribute of the On/Off cluster, set to 0 when OnOff is TRUE and OnTime is 0 by Level Control commands. Not fully defined here.

references.name: INVALID_IN_STATE status code
references.description: A standard status code used when a device with DeadFrontBehavior is in an 'Off' state and receives Invoke/Write interactions. Defined in Core Spec.

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attribute for cluster version, mentioned as determining the highest revision number (not defined here).

references.name: Level Control Cluster
references.description: External cluster that can send commands affecting On/Off cluster attributes like OnOff, OnTime, OffWaitTime (not defined in this section).

references.name: OnOff attribute
references.description: Attribute of the On/Off cluster, indicating the current on/off state of the device (not fully defined in this section).

references.name: Off command
references.description: Command of the On/Off cluster to turn the device off (not fully defined in this section).

references.name: On command
references.description: Command of the On/Off cluster to turn the device on (not fully defined in this section).

references.name: Toggle command
references.description: Command of the On/Off cluster to toggle the device's on/off state (not fully defined in this section).

references.name: Data Model Spec
references.description: Base types (map8, enum8, bool, uint16) are defined in the Matter Data Model Specification.

references.name: Scenes Management server cluster
references.description: A cluster mentioned in the context of Scene Table Extensions, but not defined in this section.

references.name: ExtensionFieldSetStruct
references.description: A structure type mentioned as part of the Scene Table, but not defined in this section.

references.name: Scenes Cluster
references.description: A server cluster that, if implemented on the same endpoint, requires the OnOff attribute to be part of its ExtensionFieldSetStruct for scene table extensions.

references.name: MoveToLevel(WithOnOff) command
references.description: A command (likely from the Level Control cluster) that, when received, can cause the GlobalSceneControl attribute to be set to TRUE.

references.name: RecallScene command
references.description: A command (likely from the Scenes Cluster) that, when received, can cause the GlobalSceneControl attribute to be set to TRUE.

references.name: StartUpOnOffEnum
references.description: An enumeration type that defines the specific startup behaviors for the StartUpOnOff attribute.

references.name: OffOnly feature
references.description: A feature that, if supported, causes an UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND failure status response upon receipt of On or Toggle commands.

references.name: Interaction Model
references.description: Core specification for command invocation and status responses, such as UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND, which is sent when a command is not supported due to feature configuration.

references.name: EffectIdentifierEnum
references.description: An enumeration type that defines the various fading effects available for the OffWithEffect command, not defined within this section.

references.name: DelayedAllOffEffectVariantEnum
references.description: An enumeration type that defines specific variants for the 'Delayed All Off' effect, referenced by the EffectVariant field of the OffWithEffect command.

references.name: DyingLightEffectVariantEnum
references.description: An enumeration type that defines specific variants for the 'Dying Light' effect, referenced by the EffectVariant field of the OffWithEffect command.

references.name: GlobalSceneControl attribute
references.description: An attribute within the On/Off cluster that dictates whether global scene storage and recall are active, influencing the behavior of OffWithEffect and OnWithRecallGlobalScene commands.

references.name: Scenes Management cluster
references.description: A separate Matter application cluster responsible for managing and recalling scenes. It is interacted with by the OffWithEffect and OnWithRecallGlobalScene commands for global scene operations.

references.name: OnOffControlBitmap
references.description: A bitmap data type used in the OnWithTimedOff command's OnOffControl field to convey operational parameters, including the 'AcceptOnlyWhenOn' bit.
